utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey william so i see that you injured your knee could you tell me a bit about what happened

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i thought it was a good idea to go to the trampoline park with my wife and heard a snap and instant pain when i was on the trampoline

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and so do do you go to the trampoline park often

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't and i think that's the issue

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so this is the first time you'd ever done something like that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright i just wan na get like a few more details do you like work out regularly was this for fun or for like an exercise class

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no it's just for fun

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and do you have like a regular exercise regimen at all

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: try to not consistent with it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay no that's fine this is not a place of judgment i just i'm just trying to understand like if a little bit more of the background okay so we went to the trampoline park which knee where did you hurt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my right

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay your right knee and so based on like your inside and outside which part hurts more

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's kind of the outside of the knee

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so the lateral aspect not a problem and you said that you heard a pop when you landed right

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay have you taken anything for the pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: just tylenol

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so did the tylenol help

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not really

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and so hmmm when does it have you tried anything else like did you ice it use heat anything like that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i just kinda elevated it and that was about it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay that's fine and how long ago did this happen

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a week ago

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright so you put up with the pain for a week that's i'm proud of you for that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't like to come to the doctors

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: excuse me i feel rejected right now why why would you say something like that it's fine but you know i i like it when you're not here either because that means that you're doing a good job so let's see about like getting your knee fixed up but i want to talk about some of the other issues that you have first so we know that you have hypertension right and i discussed maybe you getting a blood pressure cuff because i needed you to measure those a bit more regularly did you get the cuff

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: what no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no okay that's fine have you measured your blood pressure recently at all

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay are you still taking your medication because i see you're on twenty milligrams of lisinopril

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah most days i remember

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: ra i'm not suspicious but we're just gon na go with that okay because looking at your vitals it it is of still a bit high i'm not really comfortable i see like a hundred and eighty over you know eighty and

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's not where we wan na be so i wonder if we might need to adjust your medication but let's talk about your diabetes how are your blood sugars

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think they're little bit better

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay what makes you think that have you been taking them

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i check it you know most days again kinda when i remember

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay how often are you checking it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i would say four five times a week

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay you might wan na switch to maybe two or three times a day but you know that's something i'm glad that you are measuring are you taking your metformin

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and then i think we have you on five hundred milligrams correct

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright how is your diet

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: kinda the same as my exercise decent could be better

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright and okay not a problem so what is your do you are you measuring your calories at all looking at your macros are you just eating what you feel like

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah just trying to watch what i eat more than anything but nothing specific

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and are you do you know if you're particularly focused on your salt like is it like a low salt diet

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no just trying to watch my carbs a little bit more but not counting or anything

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so like is it a specific diet i just wan na make sure like are you on like the keto diet

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright not a problem so if you do n't mind i'm gon na go ahead and do a quick physical exam as i mentioned before your blood pressure is a little high as i listen to your heart i do like it's got a nice regular rate and rhythm i do n't appreciate any murmur when i listen to your lungs they sound clear bilaterally i would like to look at your knee though so when i press here on the outside does it hurt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: little bit

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and then when i press on the inside does it hurt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright can you bend your knee and straighten it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i can i think that's all i can i can bend

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm alright so are you having problems walking can you bear weight

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i can but i have a little bit of a limp

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so do you mind getting up and walking for me really quickly alright so i do notice that there is a slight gait like there is a small sorry you are correcting you do have a limp i i am a little worried about that but it's probably it's probably the superficial when i'm looking at your knee i do notice some like ecchymosis and edema that just means bruising and swelling along the lateral aspect of your knee i do n't notice any effusion and it looks like you have a decent range of motion but i do understand that you know you are experiencing pain with some movement okay i'm gon na go ahead and order an x-ray and when you come back we can have that discussion alright so i reviewed the results of your right knee x-ray which showed no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality so let's talk about my assessment and plan alright so for your first problem of right knee pain i think you have a lateral a lateral ligament strain i wan na prescribe some meloxicam which is gon na be fifteen milligrams daily for pain and swelling i'm gon na refer you to physical therapy to help strengthen the muscles around the area and to prevent further injury if you're still having pain we can do further imaging imaging but like this is a common injury that tends to heal on its own for your second problem with hypertension i wan na continue the lisinopril at twenty milligrams and order an echo i am concerned that we might not be getting your blood pressure to where we need it to be so we might have to do some medication modification for your third problem with diabetes i wan na order an a1c i know that you said you have been measuring your blood sugars but i think this would give us a better image of what's been happening long term and i also wan na order a lipid panel in case we need to make any adjustments to that medication as well do you have any questions

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright awesome

detail_list.summary: The doctor initiates the visit to evaluate the patient's right knee injury.
The patient sustained a right knee injury at a trampoline park, hearing a snap followed by instant pain.
The doctor asks if the patient visits the trampoline park frequently.
The patient confirms he does not visit the trampoline park often.
The doctor asks if the patient exercises regularly.
The patient states the trampoline activity was for fun, not exercise.
The patient admits he is inconsistent with his exercise regimen.
The doctor asks which part of the knee is painful.
The patient reports pain on the lateral aspect of the knee.
The doctor asks if the patient has taken any medication for the pain.
The patient has been taking Tylenol for the pain.
The patient reports that Tylenol has not provided significant relief.
The doctor asks if the patient has tried ice or heat.
The patient has only elevated the knee and has not tried ice or heat.
The doctor asks how long ago the injury occurred.
The injury occurred one week ago.
Hypertension
The doctor asks if the patient purchased a blood pressure cuff as previously recommended.
The patient did not purchase a blood pressure cuff.
The doctor asks if the patient has measured his blood pressure recently.
The patient has not measured his blood pressure recently.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking his lisinopril 20 mg.
The patient reports taking his lisinopril most days.
The patient's blood pressure is 180/80 mmHg.
Diabetes
The doctor asks how the patient's blood sugars have been.
The doctor asks how often the patient checks his blood glucose.
The patient checks his blood glucose 4 to 5 times per week.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking his metformin 500 mg.
The patient confirms he is taking his metformin.
The doctor asks about the patient's diet.
The patient tries to watch what he eats but does not follow a specific diet plan.
The patient tries to limit his carbohydrate intake.
The doctor performs a physical exam: heart has regular rate and rhythm without murmurs, lungs are clear bilaterally.
The doctor asks if the patient has pain when pressing on the outside of the knee.
The patient reports pain when the lateral aspect of the knee is palpated.
The doctor asks if the patient has pain when pressing on the inside of the knee.
The patient reports no pain on the inside of the knee.
The doctor asks the patient to bend and straighten his knee.
The doctor asks if the patient can bear weight on the knee.
The patient can bear weight but has a limp.
The doctor observes ecchymosis and edema along the lateral aspect of the knee, no effusion, and decent range of motion with pain.
X-rays show no fracture or bony abnormality.
The doctor assesses the injury as a lateral ligament strain.
The doctor prescribes meloxicam 15 mg daily.
The doctor refers the patient to physical therapy.
The doctor advises the patient to continue lisinopril 20 mg and orders an echocardiogram.
The doctor orders an A1c test and a lipid panel.
The doctor instructs the patient to measure blood glucose 2 to 3 times per day and continue metformin 500 mg.
